title: Nav Sangha
text:
Hi, I’m Nav Sangha.
I turn raw data into automated solutions and actionable business insights.
I am a Data Professional specializing in building robust data pipelines, exploratory analysis, and interactive reporting. I bridge the gap between complex technical architecture and strategic business operations.
My Core Stack: Python, Pandas, SQL, BeautifulSoup, and Quarto.



Featured Project: Data Analysis: Automated Data Pipeline
I built an automated pipeline using Python, BeautifulSoup, and Pandas to extract and structure the data from a 3rd party website.
👉 Read the full case study here

title: Data Analysis: Automated Data Pipeline
text: We needed a reliable way to track the list of largest companies in the US by revenue, but the data was on another 3rd party website.

title: Data Analysis: Automated Data Pipeline
text: We needed a reliable way to track the list of largest companies in the US by revenue, but the data was on another 3rd party website.

title: Data Analysis: Automated Data Pipeline
text:
Web Scraping & Cleaning
I built an automated pipeline using Python, BeautifulSoup, and Pandas to extract and structure the data.

import pandas as pd
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup
import requests

url = 'https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_largest_companies_in_the_United_States_by_revenue'

page = requests.get(url, headers = {'User-Agent': 'CustomBot (xyz@gmail.com)'})

soup = BeautifulSoup(page.text, 'html')

## print(soup)

soup.find_all('table')[0]

soup.find('table', class_ = 'wikitable sortable')

table = soup.find_all('table')[0]

## print(table)

company_titles = table.find_all('th')

company_titles

company_table_titles = [title.text.strip() for title in company_titles]

## print(company_table_titles)

df = pd.DataFrame(columns = company_table_titles)

df

column_data = table.find_all('tr')

for row in column_data[1:]:
    row_data = row.find_all('td')
    individual_row_data = [data.text.strip() for data in row_data]

    length = len(df)
    df.loc[length] = individual_row_data

df

## df.to_csv(r'/Users/nav/WebScraping for Output\Companies.csv', index = False)
